Task: Select a conventional ML pilot and an LLM batch pilot; record team allocation, current baselines, batch completion window, and Azure OpenAI RPM/TPM quota
Done criteria: Owners, weekly hours, baseline measures, processing SLA, p95 tokens per document, required RPM/TPM, and success metrics are approved

Task: Submit one infrastructure-as-code request for the MLflow Container App, PostgreSQL, Blob artifact container, Key Vault, Entra application, network, ACR permissions, Azure ML compute, Container Apps workers, Redis container, and monitoring
Deliverable: Filed ticket with full resource and RBAC list
Done criteria: Ticket is acknowledged with an ETA and no later phase depends on an unrequested resource

Task: Build a pinned custom MLflow 3 image with PostgreSQL and Azure Blob dependencies; deploy it to Container Apps with one minimum replica
Deliverable: Versioned MLflow image and deployment definition
Done criteria: Image is referenced by digest, health checks pass, the version endpoint is monitored, and local and Azure ML clients can log a test run

Task: Configure Azure Database for PostgreSQL as the backend and Blob through MLflow's artifact proxy; enable PostgreSQL point-in-time restore and Blob soft delete
Done criteria: Runs, models, prompts, traces, and artifacts survive a Container App replacement and clients require no direct Blob credentials

Task: Protect the MLflow UI and API with Entra ID for users and workload identities; store required secrets in Key Vault
Done criteria: All unauthenticated requests fail; five team members, GitHub Actions, local clients, and Azure ML jobs have least-privilege tested access

Task: Create backup, restore, upgrade, database migration, and rollback runbooks; assign a named platform owner and deputy
Done criteria: A point-in-time database restore and artifact read are tested; a pre-migration backup and rollback procedure are demonstrated in test

Task: Add MLflow 3 logging for Git SHA, dataset version, parameters, metrics, artifacts, environment, and lineage to the conventional ML pilot
Done criteria: A local and remote training run records all required lineage in the self-hosted server

Task: Convert redacted or synthetic Excel smoke-test cases into JSONL or CSV under version control; keep sensitive cases in a versioned Blob data asset
Done criteria: Dataset has a schema and at least 10 cases, with data classification and storage location documented

Task: Extract prompts into Git files and register the reviewed content as immutable MLflow prompt versions
Deliverable: Git prompt source and MLflow prompt versions
Done criteria: Each prompt version links to its Git SHA and can be resolved by exact version and candidate alias

Task: Define Gitflow branch policy and a release manifest covering code SHA, dataset, image digest, exact model and prompt versions, task contract, queue, configuration, and approver
Done criteria: The team agrees that branches govern code while MLflow aliases and manifests govern artifact promotion

Task: Add Pandera schema and quality checks to extraction and training; log reports to MLflow
Done criteria: Checks run on dataset creation and before training, failures stop the pipeline, and reports are visible in MLflow

Task: Package preprocessing as a scikit-learn Pipeline bundled with the MLflow model
Done criteria: Inference applies the packaged transformations without separate learned-parameter files

Task: Move candidate training into an Azure ML job definition stored in Git and connect it to self-hosted MLflow
Deliverable: Azure ML job YAML in Git
Done criteria: Scale-to-zero compute runs remotely and logs complete lineage to self-hosted MLflow

Task: Register pilot training data as an Azure ML data asset with an immutable Blob path
Done criteria: Data asset is queryable by name and version and the underlying path cannot be overwritten

Task: Register the first passing model and prompt versions; implement candidate, staging, and champion aliases mutated only by CI
Done criteria: A GitHub Environment approval moves an alias, and the release manifest records the exact resolved immutable version

Task: Build MLflow and application images in GitHub Actions and push immutable digests to ACR
Done criteria: A merge produces scanned images and records their digests without using mutable tags for deployment

Task: Deploy the pilot to test with workload identity, run smoke tests, and require GitHub Environment approval for production
Done criteria: Deployment uses the exact image, model, and prompt versions from the approved manifest without VS Code steps

Task: Implement rollback of the exact prior release manifest, including aliases, image, configuration, and Function package where applicable
Done criteria: A compatible prior release is restored in under 15 minutes and the deployed exact versions are verified

Task: Configure dashboards and alerts for MLflow, PostgreSQL, application failures, latency, token use, and budgets
Done criteria: Alerts cover MLflow health, database capacity, error rate, latency p95, token use, Azure actual and forecast spend; log ingestion caps are emergency-only

Task: Deploy self-hosted Redis on Container Apps as Celery broker and result backend: one minimum replica, 1 GB memory, versioned list keys, TLS, and a health endpoint
Done criteria: Health check passes, task publish and dequeue complete under 10ms, result retrieval and idempotency key operations work, and 7-day key expiry is configured and tested

Task: Build Celery workers for the 30000-document workload with late acknowledgement, rejection on worker loss, bounded retry with jitter, idempotency, quota-aware concurrency, and Redis list key consumption
Done criteria: Load test processes representative tasks without duplicate side effects, uncontrolled 429 retries, or lost terminal status; results and idempotency keys are written to Redis with 7-day TTL

Task: Publish tasks directly from the controlled uploader and from a Celery Beat reconciliation schedule that scans for unprocessed documents and republishes them idempotently
Done criteria: Every eligible document receives one stable idempotency key; Beat reconciliation detects and republishes missing work without duplicate effects; Redis restart recovery is tested end-to-end

Task: Implement release-specific Redis list keys and a blue-green worker workflow: deploy new revision, smoke-test its key, switch producer route, drain old key, then deactivate old revision
Done criteria: Old and new revisions never consume new tasks from the same Redis key; deactivation is blocked until LLEN reports zero on the old key; unsupported task contracts are rejected and logged

Task: Configure Celery warm shutdown, late acknowledgement, low prefetch, task time limits, and Container Apps termination grace; test scale-in, forced termination, and Redis restart recovery via reconciliation
Done criteria: Scale-in finishes or safely redelivers tasks, duplicate delivery has no duplicate side effect, no task depends on container-local state, and reconciliation recovers lost queue state after Redis restart

Task: Configure KEDA Redis list scaling with zero minimum replicas and a quota-derived maximum; monitor LLEN and active workers
Done criteria: Workers process 30000 documents inside the agreed window, scale to zero, and never exceed the Azure OpenAI concurrency budget

Task: Test rollback of producer routing and worker revision with compatible and incompatible task contracts using Redis key routing
Done criteria: Compatible rollback completes under 15 minutes; incompatible tasks are logged and skipped until explicitly transformed and replayed

Task: Capture prediction ID, task contract, producer and worker release, exact prompt and model versions, latency, token use, retry, Redis LLEN, and failure reason in structured telemetry; build Azure Monitor health dashboards and KQL workbooks
Done criteria: Every prediction is traceable; dashboards cover MLflow, Redis, PostgreSQL, and worker health; alerts fire for LLEN growth, stale active revisions, exhausted retries, and sustained 429 responses

Task: Schedule daily mini-evaluation and weekly full evaluation jobs against recent production samples
Done criteria: Jobs produce versioned MLflow evaluation reports and each alert has a documented team response

Task: Export structured Teams feedback into Blob or Table storage linked to prediction IDs
Done criteria: At least one week of classified and access-controlled feedback is linked to predictions

Task: Record every production release with MLflow run, evaluation, Git SHA, dataset, image digest, exact artifacts, task contract, queue, approver, and timestamp
Done criteria: At least three releases, including one worker queue transition, are fully traced end-to-end

Task: Review success criteria, team load, Azure costs, Redis operations, recovery tests, and remaining gaps
Done criteria: Team agrees what to keep, change, defer, and who owns ongoing MLflow, Redis, and PostgreSQL operations

Task: When needed, request GPU quota, deploy the optional GPU compute Bicep module, and run a multi-GPU fine-tuning job that logs to self-hosted MLflow
Deliverable: GPU training job definition in Git
Done criteria: GPU quota is approved, the optional GPU Bicep module deploys a GPU cluster attached to the existing Azure ML workspace, a multi-node or multi-GPU job completes, logs parameters, metrics, and artifacts to self-hosted MLflow, and the cluster scales to zero
